item.title: Rethinking the PAPR Pitfall in Deep Learning-Based Semantic Communication Systems
item.description: The high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) remains a persistent challenge in orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) systems. Traditional PAPR analysis assumes that the OFDM time-domain signal follows a complex Gaussian distribution, based on the summation of quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM)-modulated symbols. However, this assumption no longer holds in semantic communication (SemCom) systems due to their non-Gaussian outputs from the deep learning (DL) networks. In this paper, we investigate the PAPR characteristic in SemCom systems and propose a simple yet effective DL network structure of semantic encoder. Specifically, we find that the output features produced by the DL-based semantic encoder follow an $\alpha $ -stable distribution ( $\alpha \lt 2$ ), exhibiting heavier tails compared to the Gaussian distribution ( $\alpha =2$ ). Based on this finding, we propose a novel PAPR analysis framework that shifts the focus from conventional peak-to-average metrics to a statistical characterization of the tail behavior of signal peaks. Within this framework, we derive a theoretical upper bound on the peak envelope of OFDM signals, indicating that high-PAPR events are more likely in SemCom than in traditional communication systems. To address this, we reshape the heavy-tailed output of semantic encoders into near-Gaussian signals by substituting the final convolutional or nonlinear layer with a fully connected layer. Extensive simulations on various structures and CIFAR-10 dataset show that our proposed structure can achieve substantial PAPR reduction (up to 12.64 dB) with negligible degradation in semantic performance (down to 1.07 dB) compared with baseline.
item.dc:source: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications - new TOC

item.title: Hierarchical Codebook Design for RIS-Based Transmit Arrays
item.description: This paper investigates hierarchical codebook design for Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface based Transmit Arrays (TA-RIS) architectures operating under practical hardware constraints. We consider a transmissive RIS illuminated by one or more feeders, where the elements can only implement phase shifts and cannot be deactivated, i.e., cannot absorb the signal. A two-dimensional hierarchical codebook tailored to this architecture is proposed to enable efficient beam management and user localization without explicit channel state information estimation. To generate the codewords, we formulate a beampattern optimization problem that supports both wide and narrow beams and present two discrete-phase implementations: direct quantization of an unquantized interior-point solution and a Genetic Algorithm (GA) that operates natively in the discrete domain. A symmetry-based strategy is also introduced to reduce codebook storage requirements. Simulation results evaluate the performance of the algorithms considering 1, 2 and 3 bits phase quantization, and show that for RIS with a moderate number of elements and quantized phase shifts, GA emerge as a promising alternative to implement the desired codewords and construct a hierarchical codebook.
item.dc:source: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications - new TOC
